title: Project-local dependency artifacts for local development (frontend-only)

requirements.summary: Add an English local development guide covering local run steps and how to keep dependency/artifact files inside the project folder
acceptanceCriteria: A new documentation file exists in the repo (e.g., LOCAL_DEV.md or README-local.md) written in English.
The guide includes steps to: install DFX, start the local replica, deploy canisters, install frontend deps, and run the frontend dev server.
The guide explicitly explains where dependency/artifact files will be created on disk (e.g., node_modules, pnpm store, .dfx) and how to configure them so they live under the project directory.
The guide includes a troubleshooting section for common local issues (missing DFX, missing Node/pnpm, canister deploy failures).

file_operations.description: Create an English local development guide with copy-paste commands for installing DFX, starting the local replica, deploying canisters, installing frontend dependencies using a project-local package store, and running the frontend dev server. Explicitly document on-disk locations for project-local artifacts (e.g., frontend/node_modules, frontend/.pnpm-store, and ../.dfx) and include a troubleshooting section for common issues.

requirements.summary: Add repo-checked-in configuration to force the frontend package manager store/cache to be project-local
acceptanceCriteria: A repo-checked-in configuration file is added/updated to force the package manager store to be located in a project-local directory (e.g., a .pnpm-store folder under the repo).
The local dev guide documents the exact commands to install using that project-local store.
After running the documented install steps, the dependency store/cache directory is created inside the project folder (not only in a global home directory).

file_operations.description: Add a repo-checked-in package-manager configuration that forces a project-local package store/cache directory under the frontend folder (e.g., configure pnpm store-dir to ./ .pnpm-store so installs do not rely on a user-level global store).

Add/ensure ignore rules for project-local dependency artifacts such as frontend/node_modules and frontend/.pnpm-store so they are not committed, while still allowing the configuration files to be checked in.
Document the exact install commands that use the checked-in project-local store/cache configuration (including how to verify the store directory was created under the project folder).

requirements.summary: Add a single repo script to verify local prerequisites and the presence of project-local dependency directories, with actionable remediation output
acceptanceCriteria: A new script is added under a scripts/ (or equivalent) directory that can be run from the repo root.

The script checks for: dfx availability, Node availability, package manager availability, and existence of required project-local directories once initialized (e.g., .dfx and the frontend dependency directories).
If something is missing, the script exits non-zero and prints actionable English instructions to fix it.
If everything is present, the script exits zero and prints a short success summary.

file_operations.description: Create a runnable verification script that can be executed from the repo root (e.g., via `node frontend/scripts/check-local-dev.mjs`). The script should check for availability of dfx, Node, and a package manager (prefer pnpm, allow npm fallback), and verify presence of project-local directories after initialization (e.g., ../.dfx, frontend/node_modules, frontend/.pnpm-store). On missing items, exit non-zero and print clear English commands/instructions; on success, exit zero with a brief summary.
Add documentation for the single verification command/script (how to run it from the repo root, what it checks, and what to do when it reports missing prerequisites or directories).
